task: Write a Python function nth_smallest(nums, n) returning the nth smallest distinct value in the list, counting from 1. Return None when n is less than 1 or exceeds the number of distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all the requirements, but it fails to name any edge cases.
output:
```python
def nth_smallest(nums, n):
    nums = sorted(list(set(nums)))
    if len(nums) < n or n <= 0:
        return None
    return nums[n-1]
```
trace: I approached this task by first removing duplicate values in the list using the set() function and converting it back to a list for sorting. I then implemented a simple conditional statement that checks if n is within the valid range (greater than 0 and less than or equal to the number of unique elements) before returning the nth smallest element. An edge case to handle is when n is less than 1 or exceeds the number of distinct values in the list, in which case I return None.
